Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI/ML Engineering falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of Master Data Management. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is AI/ML Engineering truly out-of-scope for the Director of Master Data Management? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list AI/ML Engineering as a core or recommended competency for the Director of Master Data Management. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms AI/ML Engineering falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of Master Data Management. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: AI/ML Engineering is outside the competency scope of the Director of Master Data Management. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
